what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard breakdown item that documents mobile / iOS usability as a first-class project constraint, listing specified user realities, implied design principles, and proposed feature additions.
observations: Title is 'BD-I · Breakdown · Mobile / iOS usability (a first-class constraint)'
Frontmatter includes id, address, type=block, source=claude_code, state=current, author_session, channel=dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-11da929d
SPECIFIED section: everything is on his iPhone; must be convenient and non-obtrusive; small screen; user is not a product designer

IMPLIED section: thumb-zone design, minimal chrome, progressive disclosure, gesture-first with button fallback, mobile Safari performance, installable PWA, offline tolerance + sync, large tap targets, haptics, iOS scroll/selection conventions, readable typography/contrast, light/dark mode
Crucial verification note: much can only be truly verified on his actual device via a 'needs-Tim loop', never assumed-green

ADDITIONS section: PWA install, haptic feedback on level changes + submit, voice input for comment text, swipe gestures (e.g., delete draft), reading progress, remember scroll position per document, one-handed mode
standouts: Explicit 'needs-Tim loop' requiring real-device verification rather than assumed-green
iPhone-first / mobile Safari framing as a first-class constraint
Concrete additions bundle: PWA install, haptics, voice input, swipe gestures, reading progress, scroll memory, one-handed mode
conventions: Follows noticeboard item YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author, channel, links, timestamps, and history
Uses markdown headings and SPECIFIED / IMPLIED / ADDITIONS structure
Links to a parent item via part_of
concerns: ADDITIONS mix high-level constraints with specific implementation features without prioritization or scope
Verification is gated on a specific individual ('needs-Tim loop'), creating a potential bottleneck
No explicit accessibility requirements beyond contrast/tap targets
No acceptance criteria, metrics, or timeline for verifying usability
Voice input and haptics may require OS permissions not mentioned
notes: A breakdown item that frames iPhone/mobile usability as a first-class constraint, enumerates implied UX principles, and adds concrete features, while insisting real-device validation must go through Tim.
questions: Who is 'he' / 'Tim' and what is his exact role?
What is the parent item board://item-11da929d and how does this breakdown relate to it?
What product or document is being designed?
What does 'level changes' refer to for haptic feedback?
How is the 'needs-Tim loop' operationalized in the workflow?
What is the scope and timeline for these usability requirements?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a breakdown of mobile/iOS usability constraints and proposed design responses.
intention_signals: It is trying to elevate mobile/iOS usability from an afterthought to a primary design constraint and to give the team a concrete, verifiable checklist of UX patterns, while flagging that final validation must happen on the user's actual iPhone.
novelty: The explicit 'needs-Tim loop' for real-device verification and the framing of mobile usability as a first-class ledger constraint, paired with a dense bundle of iOS-specific additions (PWA, haptics, voice input, one-handed mode).
contribution: Contributes a structured set of iPhone-centric usability constraints, implied design heuristics, and candidate features that a fused system should preserve when reasoning about mobile UX requirements and verification workflows.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard breakdown item titled 'BD-I · Breakdown · Mobile / iOS usability (a first-class constraint)' that records iPhone-first usability constraints, implied design principles, and proposed additions such as PWA install, haptics, voice input, and one-handed mode, noting that real-device verification depends on a 'needs-Tim' loop.
intention_for_embedding: To capture mobile/iOS usability as a first-class design constraint and guide implementation choices with verifiable, device-specific feedback.
